info.title: Arazzo Workflow Control Plane API
info.summary: Inspect and remediate durable Arazzo workflow runs.
info.description: A REST surface over the Arazzo durability control plane (`IWorkflowManagementClient`, plan §11): list and inspect runs, resume a faulted run, cancel a run, and reap terminal ones. It is the workflow-level analogue of dead-letter inspection and redelivery. Every control action takes a single-owner lease and writes under optimistic concurrency, so concurrent operators — or an operator and a worker — cannot conflict. This document is the contract a CLI (`arazzo-runs`) and other clients are generated against; an implementation maps each operation onto the corresponding `IWorkflowManagementClient` method over a chosen durability store.

servers.description: The control-plane host (a service the operator deploys alongside its durability store).

tags.name: runs
tags.description: Visibility and remediation for durable workflow runs.

get.description: Returns a page of runs matching the visibility query. Backed by the store's wait/visibility index (the same index Tier 2 uses for wakeups), so the filters are answered server-side.

PURGE.description: Reaps completed and cancelled runs last updated strictly before `olderThan`, up to `limit`. Non-terminal runs (including faulted ones, which are terminal-but-recoverable) are never purged.

get.description: Returns a run's management detail — status, cursor, wait/fault detail and etag — from its authoritative checkpoint.

post.description: Resumes a faulted run, re-executing it from its last checkpoint (the faulted step). Only a run in the `Faulted` state can be resumed. Returns the run's new detail on success. An implementation MAY process the resume asynchronously and instead return `202 Accepted`.

200.description: The run was resumed; the body is its new detail.

post.description: Cancels a non-terminal run, marking it `Cancelled` (any fault record is kept for post-mortem visibility). Returns the run's new detail on success.

200.description: The run was cancelled; the body is its new detail.

bearerAuth.description: The control plane is privileged; protect it with a bearer token (e.g. an operator/management identity).

StatusQuery.name: status
StatusQuery.description: Restrict to runs in this lifecycle status.

WorkflowIdQuery.name: workflowId
WorkflowIdQuery.description: Restrict to runs of this workflow.

LimitQuery.name: limit
LimitQuery.description: The maximum number of runs to return (or delete, for purge).

PageTokenQuery.name: pageToken
PageTokenQuery.description: An opaque continuation token from a previous page's `nextPageToken`, for paging.

OlderThanQuery.name: olderThan
OlderThanQuery.description: Reap terminal runs last updated strictly before this instant (RFC 3339 date-time).

NotFound.description: No run with that id exists.

Conflict.description: The action does not apply in the run's current state, or the run is held by another owner (lease) or was changed concurrently.

notFaulted.summary: Resume requested for a run that is not faulted

value.title: Run is not resumable
value.detail: Run 'adopt-42' is Completed; only a Faulted run can be resumed.

leaseHeld.summary: Another owner holds the run's lease

value.title: Run is busy
value.detail: Run 'adopt-42' is currently held by another owner.

WorkflowRunStatus.description: A run's lifecycle status. `Faulted` is terminal-but-recoverable (awaiting remediation); `Completed` and `Cancelled` are terminal.

WorkflowWaitKind.description: What a suspended run is waiting for.

dueAt.description: When a timer wait becomes due (set for `Timer`).

channel.description: The channel a message wait listens on (set for `Message`).

correlationId.description: The correlation id a message wait matches, if any.

attempt.description: The attempt count at the fault.

WorkflowRunSummary.description: A run as projected into the visibility index (one entry in a page).

errorType.description: The fault error, if the run is/was faulted.

WorkflowRunPage.description: A page of runs matching a visibility query.

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

WorkflowRunDetail.description: A run's management detail, read from its authoritative checkpoint.

cursor.description: The state-machine index of the next step to run.

wait.description: Why the run is suspended, if it is.

fault.description: The fault record if the run is (or was) faulted.

etag.description: The optimistic-concurrency token the checkpoint was read at.

ResumeMode.description: How to resume a run. Only `RetryFaultedStep` is supported today; `Rewind`, `Skip`, and `StatePatch` are reserved.

ResumeRequest.description: How to resume a faulted run.

CancelRequest.description: A request to cancel a run.

reason.description: An operator-supplied reason for the cancellation (recorded for audit).
